title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
summary: SpaceX aborted the launch of its Starship Flight 13 on July 16 due to engine ignition failures. This marks another delay in the vehicle's testing and development process.

title: SpaceX launches 21 satellites for military data network
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 21 satellites for York Space Systems, contributing to the Space Development Agency's Tranche 1 Transport Layer constellation. This marks half of the planned constellation now in orbit.

title: Nature study identifies a new way to spot nuclear weapons in orbit
summary: A study in Nature Astronomy proposes a new satellite design to detect thermonuclear warheads in orbit. The research is partly funded by unspecified sources.

title: Defense Innovation Unit seeks commercial path to deliver electrical power from orbit
summary: The Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) is soliciting commercial solutions to deliver electrical power from orbit by fiscal 2030, aiming to advance long-studied space-based solar power technology for military use. This initiative seeks to transition theoretical concepts into practical applications for national defense.

title: Building the infrastructure for a shifting space economy with Massi Ladovaz
summary: SpinLaunch CEO Massi Ladovaz discusses reimagining spacecraft launch methods and the challenges of building satellite constellations to support a shifting space economy.

title: Trump’s pick to lead Space Force defends plan to more than double budget
summary: Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess, Trump's nominee to lead the U.S. Space Force, defended plans to more than double the Space Force's budget during a Senate Armed Services Committee confirmation hearing on July 16.

title: Office of Space Commerce makes its case for mission authorization
summary: The Office of Space Commerce advocated for its mission authorization proposal to offer regulatory clarity for companies while minimizing bureaucratic hurdles. The proposal aims to address novel space activities under a streamlined framework.

title: Bluestaq Launches BLUESTAQ / ARQ Defense-Grade Data Infrastructure Now Available for Commercial, Government, and Enterprise Organizations
summary: Bluestaq launched BLUESTAQ / ARQ, a commercial data infrastructure platform designed for defense-grade use by healthcare, government, and enterprise organizations. The platform is now available for commercial and government applications.

title: Swissto12 raises $70 million to accelerate small GEO satellite production
summary: Swissto12 has secured $70 million in funding to boost its production of small geostationary (GEO) satellites in response to increasing demand. The investment will help accelerate the company's manufacturing capabilities.

title: Space Force awards Slingshot $69 million for AI-enabled training technology
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded Slingshot Aerospace a $69 million contract to develop AI-enabled training technology for simulated satellite-defense scenarios over the next 4.5 years.

title: SpaceX's Starship Flight 13 test launch aborts at last second (video)
summary: SpaceX aborted the 13th test flight of its Starship rocket just before launch on July 16. The company has not yet announced a new launch date.

title: Astronomers discover landslides on Pluto large enough to bury entire cities on Earth
summary: Astronomers have discovered landslides on Pluto large enough to bury entire cities on Earth, based on imagery from NASA's New Horizons mission. The evidence points to landslides occurring down steep crater walls on the dwarf planet.

title: Astronomers discover 1st atmosphere around a rocky Earth-like planet in the habitable zone
summary: Astronomers have detected the first atmosphere around a rocky, Earth-like planet located in the habitable zone. This discovery is significant for astrobiology and the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: SpaceX stacks massive Starship rocket ahead of today's Flight 13 test launch (video)
summary: SpaceX has stacked the two stages of its massive Starship rocket ahead of the Flight 13 test launch scheduled for July 16. This marks a significant milestone in preparation for the upcoming test flight.

title: An animated sci-fi rom-com wasn't on our wish list, but we'll be damned if 'Not Alone' doesn't look sweet, stylish, and hilarious
summary: An animated sci-fi rom-com titled 'Not Alone' is set to release in 2027, featuring a story about young rocket scientists in love and rowdy alien refugees. The film is praised for its sweet, stylish, and hilarious approach.

title: Stephen Hawking's famous 'leaky' black hole theory gets much-needed update
summary: Stephen Hawking's 'leaky' black hole theory has received a significant update after 50 years, addressing a serious limitation in his original laws of black hole mechanics.

title: A laser disappears into the Milky Way's heart | Space photo of the day for July 16, 2026
summary: A laser beam is observed disappearing into the Milky Way's center in a space photo from July 16, 2026. The image highlights a striking astronomical phenomenon.

title: SpaceX launches 21 'data transport' satellites for US military (video, photos)
summary: SpaceX launched 21 satellites for the U.S. military's 'Tranche 1 Transport Layer' constellation from California. This mission supports the military's advanced data transport capabilities.

title: NASA Welcomes Serbia as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Serbia signed the Artemis Accords, becoming the 69th signatory nation committed to peaceful space exploration. The agreement builds on Serbia's historical ties with NASA dating back to the Apollo program.

title: NASA Study Finds Near-Earth Asteroid Is Actually Comet
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory identified a near-Earth object as a comet after tracking its motion and using advanced observatories. The object had previously appeared inactive, resembling an asteroid.

title: Young Galaxy Cluster
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an image of a young galaxy cluster, MACS J0553.4-3342, from 4.4 billion years ago. The cluster consists of two merging sub-clusters, each anchored by a massive elliptical galaxy.

title: NASA Uses Subscale Aircraft to Accelerate Flight Innovation
summary: NASA is accelerating flight innovation testing by using small, remotely piloted and autonomous aircraft at the Dale Reed Subscale Flight Research Laboratory. This approach helps mature innovative ideas and reduce risk in aerospace development.

title: NASA Study of Pristine Meteorite Adds to Story of Ancient Asteroids
summary: A meteorite recovered immediately after falling to Earth in July 2024 is providing NASA scientists with new insights into ancient water, chemical evolution of primitive asteroids, and potential ingredients for life in the early solar system.

title: How NASA’s Artemis III Lander Test Will Pave Way for Moon Landings
summary: NASA plans to conduct the Artemis III lander test in 2027 to practice rendezvous and docking operations before astronauts land on the Moon in 2028. The mission will provide critical data for future uncrewed demonstrations.

title: Anil Menon Launches to Space Station
summary: Anil Menon launched to the International Space Station aboard a Soyuz rocket as part of the Soyuz MS-29 mission. The launch was witnessed by his family, including NASA astronaut candidate Anna Menon.

title: Curiosity Finds Evidence of an Ancient Sandstorm
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover discovered evidence of an ancient, intense Martian sandstorm that created layered sedimentary rocks billions of years ago. The rover captured an image of these multilayered rocks on December 12, 2024.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Provides Sweeping View of Broom Point
summary: NASA's Perseverance Rover captured a detailed view of Jezero Crater's rim using its Mastcam-Z instrument. The image, taken on May 15, 2025, highlights bright-colored rocks along the crater's slope.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Iginition
summary: Starship Flight 13's Booster 20 aborted during ignition after a planned launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT. The launch was delayed due to the abort.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for its first land recovery.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. This marks another mission to sustain operations aboard the orbiting laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Seven space launches are scheduled this week, including the 13th test flight of SpaceX's Starship and a Soyuz mission carrying a crew to the ISS.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key step toward its upcoming launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX rocket, completed a record-duration static fire test with all 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in SpaceX's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This marks another step in the region's revitalization as multiple launch providers expand their facilities.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debuts of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets. Three Falcon 9 flights are also expected from SpaceX.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital destination ahead of future missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website provides industry news related to the space sector. The specific article content is not provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm is being developed for space applications, aiming to enhance satellite servicing and maintenance. The technology could revolutionize in-space operations by providing versatile manipulation capabilities.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This initiative supports European space science by involving research institutes in space missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical center in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12, a European space company, has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round. The funds will support scaling operations to meet the demand for sovereign space hardware.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed mission authorization framework, questioning its financial feasibility. The TraCSS program was a major focus of the discussion.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: The news discusses concerns over a potential sales freeze of SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket, which could disrupt the space industry's reliance on reliable launch services. This uncertainty raises existential questions for many satellite businesses dependent on SpaceX's launch capabilities.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand their offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon's nominee for space acquisition highlighted that legacy space programs were not designed for modern acquisition reforms in the Space Force. Senators questioned the nominees on space policy during a hearing.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, bringing the total number of companies in the program to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations to offer end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns and private range access. The company will also provide test and evaluation infrastructure.

title: Lunar Outpost Joins ESA’s Moonraker Mission in 2030
summary: Lunar Outpost Europe will contribute thermal management solutions for ESA's Moonraker mission, which is scheduled for 2030 to operate in lunar orbit despite extreme temperature fluctuations.

title: Reditus Finishes ENOS Reentry Vehicle Ahead of First Flight
summary: Reditus has completed the development of its first reentry spacecraft, ENOS, ahead of its scheduled debut flight this fall.

title: Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC
summary: Reflect Orbital has received FCC clearance to launch an in-orbit mirror, despite concerns about its environmental and astronomical impact. The FCC stated that such concerns fall outside its regulatory scope.

title: Kesight and Sateliot to Explore Blockchain Frameworks for 5G NTN Security
summary: Kesight and Sateliot will collaborate with the European Space Agency to explore blockchain frameworks for securing 5G non-terrestrial networks (NTN) by detecting anomalies. The initiative aims to enhance security in satellite-based 5G communications.

title: Viasat Demonstrates Automotive Voice Call in BMW Platform
summary: Viasat demonstrated a satellite voice call integrated into a BMW vehicle's infotainment system during a 5G Automotive Association event. This showcases advancements in automotive connectivity using satellite technology.

title: AST SpaceMobile Discloses Delays to Launch Campaign, Explores Vertical Integration
summary: AST SpaceMobile has delayed its launch campaign to early 2027 and is exploring vertical integration. The company also initiated a share sale to raise additional funds.

title: Swissto12 Secures $70M in Series C Round
summary: Swissto12 has secured $70 million in a Series C funding round to expand its manufacturing capabilities. The company reports strong revenue growth and a positive trajectory.

title: Pentagon Suspends CMMC Phase II Plans, Cites Concerns With Compliance ‘Burdens’
summary: The Pentagon has suspended plans for the next phase of the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) program, citing concerns over compliance burdens. The decision involves pausing third-party audits as the program undergoes review.

title: Integration and Sovereignty: How Defense is Reshaping the Earth Observation Market
summary: Defense and security needs are increasing demand for Earth Observation (EO) data, but challenges remain in integrating this data into larger systems. European space and security organizations highlight these issues as they reshape the EO market.
company: Europe's leading space and security organizations

title: FCC to Consider Allowing Wi-Fi, Bluetooth Devices for D2D Service
summary: The FCC is considering allowing unlicensed wireless devices like Wi-Fi and Bluetooth to communicate directly with satellites for direct-to-device (D2D) service. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr proposed a new proceeding to explore this possibility.

title: South Africa’s Top ISP Herotel to Distribute Amazon Leo
summary: South Africa's leading ISP Herotel will distribute Amazon's Leo satellite connectivity to residential customers through a new partnership. Herotel is also launching its own satellite services.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense COO Kevin Bidlack Talks Growth Post-IPO in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense's COO Kevin Bidlack discusses the company's growth following its IPO during a Thursday Morning Conversation. The company, a diverse space tech firm, recently went public.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Wins Space Force Contract to Expand AI Mission Rehearsal
summary: Slingshot Aerospace secured a nearly $70 million contract with the U.S. Space Force to expand AI mission rehearsal capabilities. This is the largest contract in the company's history.
